summary: Iteration 164: Verified response.json() fix for 500 errors on Stripe/subscription endpoints. All backend APIs return valid Stripe URLs. Frontend wallet/subscriptions pages load correctly and button clicks successfully redirect to Stripe Connect onboarding and Stripe Checkout without 500 errors.

stripe_connect_onboard.details: POST /api/payments/stripe/connect/onboard returns valid Stripe Connect URL (connect.stripe.com)

api_js_uses_json_directly.description: apiRequest (line 99-106) uses response.json() as primary strategy, no clone() calls

wallet_page_loads.details: Wallet page loads correctly with total balance displayed

wallet_stripe_connect_button.details: Connect Stripe Account button found with data-testid='stripe-onboard-btn'

wallet_membership_tier_cards.details: All 4 tier cards visible (Bronze $4.99, Silver $9.99, Gold $14.99, Diamond $29.99)

subscriptions_page_loads.details: Subscriptions page loads with Memberships header

subscriptions_all_tiers.details: All 4 tier cards found with strikethrough original prices

stripe_connect_button_click.details: Clicking Connect Stripe Account redirects to connect.stripe.com successfully, NO 500 errors

subscription_button_click.details: Clicking Subscribe to Bronze redirects to checkout.stripe.com successfully, NO 500 errors

500_error_fix.description: Stripe Connect and subscription buttons no longer cause 'Request failed (500)' errors
500_error_fix.root_cause: Previous text()-first approach failed when body stream was consumed by production CDN/proxy
500_error_fix.fix: Changed to response.json() as primary body reading strategy

critical_code_review_comments: Code correctly uses response.json() directly as primary strategy in both api.js and memberPagesApi.js
No response.clone() calls found in key API files
Error handling properly returns empty object for OK responses where json() fails
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 164 verified the response.json() fix. All Stripe integration points (Connect onboard, subscription checkout) work correctly without 500 errors. The key change was using response.json() directly instead of text()-first approach which caused issues with CDN/proxy body stream consumption.
